pattern: Rewriting the balance condition as nums[i] - i being non-decreasing turns the problem into 'max-sum non-decreasing subsequence keyed by a derived value', which a Fenwick tree over compressed keys tracking the best running sum answers in one left-to-right pass.
hints: Subtract the index from each value first: once you look at b[i] = nums[i] - i, the balance condition between two chosen indices becomes simply b at the later index is at least b at the earlier index.

So you need the maximum-sum subsequence whose b-values are non-decreasing left to right; process indices in order and, for each one, find the best running sum among all earlier indices whose b-value doesn't exceed the current one.

Keep a Fenwick tree (or any structure) indexed by compressed b-values that supports 'max running sum among keys <= x' and 'update key with a new running sum'; a single element is always a valid balanced subsequence, so also compare against starting fresh at the current index.

edge_inputs.description: all values identical -- b strictly decreases so no chain extends
consecutive run where every element can chain onto the last
mixed negatives where the best answer is a single element, not a longer chain
extreme magnitude values at both ends of the range
a large dip between two big values -- the optimal chain must skip over the dip

stress.description: uniformly random values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
